hero.title: Shaping Africa’s Supply Chain Future
hero.description: AISCR (African Institute for Supply Chain Research) transforms research into concrete actions for more resilient, inclusive and sustainable supply chains across Africa

why.title: Why we exist
why.description_p1: Supply chains in Africa represent both a tremendous opportunity and a series of challenges: fragmented markets, a lack of reliable data, logistical vulnerabilities, and limited capacity. Without rigorous diagnostics and targeted actions, these weaknesses stifle growth, increase costs, and restrict access to essential goods.
why.description_p2: AISCR was created to respond to this observation: to produce applied research, train leaders and co-create solutions with local stakeholders in order to transform these challenges into levers for development.

mission.label: Mission
mission.text: We produce actionable research, train talent, and develop partnerships to make African supply chains more efficient and equitable.

vision.label: Vision
vision.text: Imagine an Africa where logistics systems are not only efficient but also designed to foster inclusive and resilient growth. Enabling everyone to benefit from equitable access to resources and markets.

pillars.title: How we work — Our Pillars
pillars.subtitle: We generate evidence, build capability, and enable collaboration to advance supply chain performance. AISCR strengthens supply chain systems by:

research.title: Applied Research
research.description: Production of field studies and analysis to guide policies and decisions.

education.title: Education & Training
education.description: Certificate programs, workshops, and scholarships to build the next generation.

solutions.title: Enterprise Solutions
solutions.description: Support for companies and institutions to implement results.

engagement.title: Events & Engagement
engagement.description: Conferences and webinars to disseminate and co-create solutions.

approach.title: Rigour + Relevance
approach.description: Nous associons méthodes quantitatives, enquêtes terrain et partenariats locaux pour produire des recommandations opérationnelles. Nos travaux privilégient l'impact mesurable : changements de politique, améliorations opérationnelles, capacités renforcées.
